motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 2°F at dawn, holding to 5°F by midmorning. Sky low and pale, the colour of old linen. A light snow — not falling so much as arriving, each flake finding its place without hurry. The lake sealed and white and undisturbed beneath it. Quarter inch of accumulation in the square by afternoon.

undercurrent.note: The channel is closed; the box holds thirteen and is done. The hum is at four minutes fifty-one seconds and still shortening — it is not fading so much as finishing, the way a note finishes when it has been sustained exactly as long as it needed to be. The undercurrent is resolving warmly: the strangeness is now furniture, not weather. Calloway and T. Aldercott are finding lodgings. Gerald set his notebook on the shelf. Agnes stayed inside. The rowboat's shadow falls correctly. These are not ends; they are the new ordinary. Level drops significantly — the pressure has become accommodation. Stage holds at 2 because the phenomenon is still present, merely settled; it will not reach 0 while the returned still gather and the hum still sounds. Next season's thread is not yet visible, only faintly felt, like a cold draft from a door that hasn't opened yet.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
season_arc.next_premise: In the spring, when the ice goes out, the lake gives something back that is not an animal — a set of keys, a coat, a pair of boots still laced — and the objects are dry, and warm, and they fit someone in town exactly, someone who has never lost them.
